perturbation.original_text: 4. NON-DISCLOSURE OBLIGATION. Unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the disclosing party, the receiving party agrees (a) not to disclose the Confidential Data; (b) use the same degree of care and diligence to protect such Confidential Data from disclosure to others as such party employs or should reasonably employ to so protect its own information of like importance (but in no event less than reasonable care); and (c) not to reproduce or copy the Confidential Data, in whole or in part, except as necessary for the evaluation or conduct of the Proposed Relationship. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the receiving party may disclose the Confidential Data, to such of the receiving party's consultants, agents and affiliates (collectively "receiving party representative") which the receiving party reasonably and in good faith believes should be <PAGE> 77 involved in the evaluation or performance of the Proposed Relationship, provided such receiving party representative is informed of this Agreement and agrees to be bound by the terms hereof, and the receiving party uses best efforts to cause the receiving party representative to observe the terms of this Agreement. The receiving party agrees that a breach of this Agreement by a receiving party representative shall constitute a breach by the receiving party. In the event that the receiving party is required by applicable law, rule, regulation or lawful order or ruling of any court, government agency or regulatory commission to disclose any Confidential Data, the receiving party agrees that it will provide the disclosing party with prompt notice of such request(s) to enable the disclosing party to seek an appropriate protective order or to take steps to protect the confidentiality of such Confidential Data.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. NON-DISCLOSURE OBLIGATION. Unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the disclosing party, the receiving party agrees (a) not to disclose the Confidential Data; (b) use the same degree of care and diligence to protect such Confidential Data from disclosure to others as such party employs or should reasonably employ to so protect its own information of like importance; and (c) not to reproduce or copy the Confidential Data, in whole or in part, except as necessary for the evaluation or conduct of the Proposed Relationship. In the event that the receiving party is required by applicable law, rule, regulation or lawful order or ruling of any court, government agency or regulatory commission to disclose any Confidential Data, the receiving party agrees that it will provide the disclosing party with prompt notice of such request(s) to enable the disclosing party to seek an appropriate protective order or to take steps to protect the confidentiality of such Confidential Data.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause regarding disclosure to consultants, agents, and affiliates ('receiving party representative') and their obligations, while retaining the overall non-disclosure obligation, the text creates a contradiction. Section 4 now states the receiving party agrees not to disclose the Confidential Data but then does not specify the conditions under which disclosure TO other parties can happen without a breach. This omission impacts the clarity of the non-disclosure obligation, especially concerning who is considered a 'receiving party'. This contradicts how the confidential data can be used in section 5
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. NO ADDITIONAL RIGHTS. The receiving party shall not have any rights or obligations respecting the Confidential Data other than those specifically set forth in this Agreement. Without limiting the generality of any other provision of this Agreement: (a) no license is hereby or otherwise granted, directly or indirectly, under any patent, copyright or other proprietary right of the disclosing party or its third party vendors; and (b) neither party shall be obligated to disclose Data to the other party or to enter into any further agreements relating to the Proposed Relationship or Data. A party may terminate discussions regarding the Proposed Relationship at any time. The receiving party shall, upon written request of the disclosing party, return to the disclosing party all Confidential Data, including all copies thereof, disclosed hereunder. The receiving party's obligations under this Agreement respecting the Confidential Data shall survive termination of said discussions.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies conditions under which the receiving party can disclose confidential data to consultants, agents, and affiliates. The changed text removes this clause, creating a situation where the receiving party agrees not to disclose the Confidential Data, but the agreement is silent about whether those parties can be involved, creating an ambiguity and impacting the clarity of the non-disclosure obligation. This contradicts section 5, which states that the receiving party shall not have any rights or obligations respecting the Confidential Data other than those specifically set forth in this Agreement, because without the explicit allowance for disclosure to representatives, any such disclosure would arguably violate the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 5. NO ADDITIONAL RIGHTS. The receiving party shall not have any rights or obligations respecting the Confidential Data other than those specifically set forth in this Agreement. Without limiting the generality of any other provision of this Agreement: (a) no license is hereby or otherwise granted, directly or indirectly, under any patent, copyright or other proprietary right of the disclosing party or its third party vendors; and (b) neither party shall be obligated to disclose Data to the other party or to enter into any further agreements relating to the Proposed Relationship or Data. A party may terminate discussions regarding the Proposed Relationship at any time. The receiving party shall, upon written request of the disclosing party, return to the disclosing party all Confidential Data, including all copies thereof, disclosed hereunder. The receiving party's obligations under this Agreement respecting the Confidential Data shall survive termination of said discussions.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. NO ADDITIONAL RIGHTS. The receiving party shall not have any rights or obligations respecting the Confidential Data other than those specifically set forth in this Agreement. Without limiting the generality of any other provision of this Agreement: (a) no license is hereby or otherwise granted, directly or indirectly, under any patent, copyright or other proprietary right of the disclosing party or its third party vendors. A party may terminate discussions regarding the Proposed Relationship at any time. The receiving party shall, upon written request of the disclosing party, return to the disclosing party all Confidential Data, including all copies thereof, disclosed hereunder. The receiving party's obligations under this Agreement respecting the Confidential Data shall survive termination of said discussions.
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(b) neither party shall be obligated to disclose Data to the other party or to enter into any further agreements relating to the Proposed Relationship or Data.' it creates an in-text contradiction with the purpose mentioned at the beginning of the document 'The parties intend to engage in discussions concerning a potential business relationship (the "Proposed Relationship"). In connection therewith, Level 3 and NNT may disclose to each other technical, financial and/or other information, material, or data which is written, oral or in any other form, electronic or otherwise (collectively "Data") which is considered confidential and proprietary.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. DISCLOSURE. The parties intend to engage in discussions concerning a potential business relationship (the "Proposed Relationship"). In connection therewith, Level 3 and NNT may disclose to each other technical, financial and/or other information, material, or data which is written, oral or in any other form, electronic or otherwise (collectively "Data") which is considered confidential and proprietary.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a clause stating that 'neither party shall be obligated to disclose Data to the other party or to enter into any further agreements relating to the Proposed Relationship or Data.' This directly aligns with the contradicted text, which states the parties 'intend to engage in discussions concerning a potential business relationship' and 'may disclose to each other...Data.' By removing the clause regarding the obligation to disclose data, the changed text creates an in-text contradiction. The original agreement explicitly states no obligation to disclose, while the introduction of the document implies an intention to potentially disclose. The removal introduces a conflict, making it uncertain whether data disclosure is intended or optional. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 6. INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. Both parties acknowledge and agree that the disclosing party and/or its third party vendors (as the case may be) own all rights, title and interest in the Confidential Data. Both parties further acknowledge and agree that the unauthorized disclosure of the Confidential Data will cause irreparable harm to the disclosing party. As a result of the unique nature of the Confidential Data, in addition to all other remedies available, the disclosing party shall be entitled to seek injunctive and other extraordinary relief in a court of competent jurisdiction in order to enforce the receiving party's obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. Both parties acknowledge and agree that the disclosing party and/or its third party vendors (as the case may be) own all rights, title and interest in the Confidential Data. Both parties further acknowledge and agree that the unauthorized disclosure of the Confidential Data will cause irreparable harm to the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the second sentence 'As a result of the unique nature of the Confidential Data, in addition to all other remedies available, the disclosing party shall be entitled to seek injunctive and other extraordinary relief in a court of competent jurisdiction in order to enforce the receiving party's obligations hereunder.' from Section 6, the Injunctive Relief clause, while acknowledging the potential for irreparable harm, doesn't explicitly state the disclosing party's right to seek injunctive relief. This creates an in-text contradiction because without the explicit statement of the right to seek injunctive relief, the clause loses its practical enforceability. While the harm is acknowledged, the immediate legal recourse isn't stated, leading to uncertainty about how the obligations under the agreement will be enforced. It therefore becomes less clear and contradictory with the title.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. Both parties acknowledge and agree that the disclosing party and/or its third party vendors (as the case may be) own all rights, title and interest in the Confidential Data. Both parties further acknowledge and agree that the unauthorized disclosure of the Confidential Data will cause irreparable harm to the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the phrase 'As a result of the unique nature of the Confidential Data, in addition to all other remedies available, the disclosing party shall be entitled to seek injunctive and other extraordinary relief in a court of competent jurisdiction in order to enforce the receiving party's obligations hereunder.' This omission creates an in-text contradiction because, while the section still acknowledges irreparable harm, it no longer explicitly states the right to seek injunctive relief. The contradicted text shows the clause as it originally was, stating clearly the Injunctive Relief rights, while the changed text omits this. Thus, the changed text now contradicts the original intent of the clause and its practical enforceability, leading to uncertainty about how the agreement's obligations will be enforced. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 7. OTHER PROVISIONS. The parties further agree that: (a) this Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Nebraska; (b) this Agreement sets forth the entire agreement and understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and none of the terms of this Agreement may be amended or modified except by a written instrument signed by both parties; (c) a party may waive any rights under this Agreement only by written waiver duly signed by such party, and no failure to exercise or delay in exercising a right under this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of such right; (d) this Agreement shall inure only to the benefit of the parties hereto, and the rights and obligations of each party under this Agreement may not be assigned or delegated without the consent of the other party; (e) no provision of this Agreement shall affect, limit or restrict either party's right to engage in any business in any place and at any time, whatsoever, provided the receiving party does not disclose the Confidential Data in violation of this Agreement; (f) each party agrees not to advertise, or otherwise make known to others, any <PAGE> 78 information regarding this Agreement or the Proposed Relationship except as may be required by law; (g) neither party makes any representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of any Data disclosed hereunder; (h) the invalidity or unenforceability of any provision of this Agreement shall not affect the validity or enforceability of any other provision of this Agreement; (i) all notices under this Agreement must be in writing and shall be deemed to have been delivered to and received by a party, and will otherwise become effective, on the date of actual delivery thereof (by personal delivery, express delivery service or certified mail) to the Notice Address of such party set forth below; (j) this Agreement may be executed in counterparts; and (k) this Agreement is dated for all reference purposes October 14, 1998.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. OTHER PROVISIONS. The parties further agree that: (a) this Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Nebraska; (b) this Agreement sets forth the entire agreement and understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and none of the terms of this Agreement may be amended or modified except by a written instrument signed by both parties; (c) a party may waive any rights under this Agreement only by written waiver duly signed by such party, and no failure to exercise or delay in exercising a right under this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of such right; (d) this Agreement shall inure only to the benefit of the parties hereto, and the rights and obligations of each party under this Agreement may not be assigned or delegated without the consent of the other party; (e) no provision of this Agreement shall affect, limit or restrict either party's right to engage in any business in any place and at any time, whatsoever, provided the receiving party does not disclose the Confidential Data in violation of this Agreement; (f) each party agrees not to advertise, or otherwise make known to others, any <PAGE> 78 information regarding this Agreement or the Proposed Relationship except as may be required by law; (g) neither party makes any representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of any Data disclosed hereunder; (h) the invalidity or unenforceability of any provision of this Agreement shall not affect the validity or enforceability of any other provision of this Agreement; (j) this Agreement may be executed in counterparts; and (k) this Agreement is dated for all reference purposes October 14, 1998.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'all notices under this Agreement must be in writing and shall be deemed to have been delivered to and received by a party, and will otherwise become effective, on the date of actual delivery thereof (by personal delivery, express delivery service or certified mail) to the Notice Address of such party set forth below;' it creates an ambiguity and contradiction. Because the way notices must be sent is not defined, it creates ambiguity about how requests, like returning the data which is referenced in section 5 must be done
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. NO ADDITIONAL RIGHTS. The receiving party shall not have any rights or obligations respecting the Confidential Data other than those specifically set forth in this Agreement. Without limiting the generality of any other provision of this Agreement: (a) no license is hereby or otherwise granted, directly or indirectly, under any patent, copyright or other proprietary right of the disclosing party or its third party vendors; and (b) neither party shall be obligated to disclose Data to the other party or to enter into any further agreements relating to the Proposed Relationship or Data. A party may terminate discussions regarding the Proposed Relationship at any time. The receiving party shall, upon written request of the disclosing party, return to the disclosing party all Confidential Data, including all copies thereof, disclosed hereunder. The receiving party's obligations under this Agreement respecting the Confidential Data shall survive termination of said discussions.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that all notices under the agreement must be in writing and delivered in a specific manner. The changed text omits this requirement. The contradicted text in section 5 requires a 'written request' for the return of confidential data. Because the changed text removes the specification of how notices must be delivered, it creates ambiguity about how the written request should be delivered, causing a contradiction. The receiving party is then unsure how they are to properly return data to the disclosing party which is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: APPENDIX "C" NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT This Agreement is made and entered into by and between NATIONAL NETWORK TECHNOLOGIES, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company, and its affiliates (collectively "NNT"), and LEVEL 3 COMMUNICATIONS, LLC a Delaware limited liability company, and its affiliates (collectively "LEVEL 3") (each being a "party" or collective "parties").
perturbation.changed_text: APPENDIX "C" NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT This Agreement is made and entered into by and between NATIONAL NETWORK TECHNOLOGIES, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company, and LEVEL 3 COMMUNICATIONS, LLC a Delaware limited liability company (each being a "party" or collective "parties").
perturbation.explanation: Removing "and its affiliates (collectively "NNT"), and its affiliates (collectively "LEVEL 3")" can create a contradiction. While the term 'affiliates' is still implied within the overall agreement's intention, its removal from this specific clause undermines the binding nature of the NDA on affiliated entities. Section 4, which outlines the non-disclosure obligations and extends them to 'receiving party representative', including affiliates, is now weakened due to this omission, creating uncertainty in enforcement and potentially impacting affiliates' compliance with the NDA. Now section 4 sounds contradictory because it uses a term not defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. NON-DISCLOSURE OBLIGATION. Unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the disclosing party, the receiving party agrees (a) not to disclose the Confidential Data; (b) use the same degree of care and diligence to protect such Confidential Data from disclosure to others as such party employs or should reasonably employ to so protect its own information of like importance (but in no event less than reasonable care); and (c) not to reproduce or copy the Confidential Data, in whole or in part, except as necessary for the evaluation or conduct of the Proposed Relationship. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the receiving party may disclose the Confidential Data, to such of the receiving party's consultants, agents and affiliates (collectively "receiving party representative") which the receiving party reasonably and in good faith believes should be <PAGE> 77 involved in the evaluation or performance of the Proposed Relationship, provided such receiving party representative is informed of this Agreement and agrees to be bound by the terms hereof, and the receiving party uses best efforts to cause the receiving party representative to observe the terms of this Agreement. The receiving party agrees that a breach of this Agreement by a receiving party representative shall constitute a breach by the receiving party. In the event that the receiving party is required by applicable law, rule, regulation or lawful order or ruling of any court, government agency or regulatory commission to disclose any Confidential Data, the receiving party agrees that it will provide the disclosing party with prompt notice of such request(s) to enable the disclosing party to seek an appropriate protective order or to take steps to protect the confidentiality of such Confidential Data.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes "and its affiliates (collectively "NNT"), and its affiliates (collectively "LEVEL 3")" from the definition of the parties to the NDA. The contradicted text (section 4) refers to 'receiving party representative', which includes 'affiliates'. Because the changed text removes the explicit inclusion of affiliates as parties bound by the agreement, it creates a contradiction with section 4, which assumes affiliates are covered, thus weakening the enforceability and clarity of the NDA with respect to affiliates.
